nameMeanings.name: mac
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Cuong, Minh, Lien, Muoi, Thanh, Tho, Tieu, Vinh, Anh, Beo, Binh, Chau.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 麥 (see   Mai   2) and 莫 (see   Mo   1).
Vietnamese (Mạc): from Chinese 莫 ‘nothing; nonexistent’; see Mo 1.

variants.name: Mai
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Thanh, Hoa, Son, Dung, Long, Binh, Minh, Thu, Hue, Huong, Thuy.  Chinese Chi, Hong, Ho, Sang, Han, Hang, Jian, Li, Man, Mei, Sen, Wei.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 麥, meaning ‘wheat’ in Chinese: (i) from Mai Qiu (麥丘), the title of a man in the state of Qi (located mainly in present-day Shandong province) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), who, despite being very old, came to celebrate the birthday of the Duke Huan of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, died 643  BC ). His descendants inherited 麥, the first part of his title, as their surname. (ii) borne by descendants of Mai Tie Zhang (麥鐵杖), a famous general during the Sui dynasty (581–618  AD ). His descendants were scattered around southern China.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 買, meaning ‘buy’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of the king Song Weizi, founder of the state of Song. (ii) borne by descendants of the Duke Dao of Xu (ruler of the state of Xu, 546–523  BC ), possibly inheriting his personal name. (iii) from the first element of the personal name Mai Zhu Chu (買朱鉏), style name of an official who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) it has also been traced back to other ethnic groups in northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Fergus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’, formed with fear ‘man’ + gus ‘vigor, force’. This was the name of an early Irish mythological figure, a valiant warrior, also of the grandfather of Saint Columba.
Scottish and English: from the Gaelic personal name Fearghus (see 1 above).

variants.name: Ferries
meanings: Scottish: according to Black, either a shortened form of MacFerries, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fearghuis ‘son of Fergus ’, or an altered form of Fergus itself. Compare Ferris .
Irish and Scottish: variant of   Ferris  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name, a variant of   Ferris  .

variants.name: Ferris
meanings: Irish and Scottish: shortened Anglicized form of Irish Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’, or from the Scottish-Gaelic form of this personal name, Fearghus (see Fergus ).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Ferrières-Saint-Hilaire, Eure and Ferrières, La Manche in Normandy, France.

variants.name: Ferguson
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fergus ‘son of Fearghus’ (see Fergus ) by substituting -son for mac-.

variants.name: Farrish
meanings: Scottish and Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fearghuis or Ó Fearghasa ‘descendant of Fearghus’ (see Fergus , and compare Ferris , Farris ).

variants.name: Farris
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of Fergus , in which the Gaelic gh sound has been dropped rather than being altered to g. Compare Farrish , Ferris .
Probably also English: variant of   Farrar  . The name is quite common in southeastern England.

nameMeanings.name: Eve
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: vernacular form of the name borne in the Bible by the first woman, created from one of Adam's ribs (Genesis 2:22). It derives, via Latin Eva, from Hebrew Havva, which is considered to be a variant of the vocabulary word hayya ‘living’ or ‘animal’. Adam gave names to all the animals (Genesis 2:19–20) and then to his wife, who was ‘the mother of all living’ (Genesis 3:20).

en.lifeSummary: Eve mac Fergus was born in 1150, in Redcastle, Highland, Scotland, United Kingdom. She had at least 1 son with Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway. She died in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Eve mac Fergus est née en 1150, à Redcastle, Highland, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway. Elle est décédée à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Eve mac Fergus wurde 1150 in Redcastle, Highland, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren. Sie hatte mit Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb in Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Eve mac Fergus è nata il nel 1150 nel Redcastle, Highland, Scozia, Regno Unito. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway. Morì nel Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Eve mac Fergusは1150年にイギリススコットランドヒグhランドレドカストルで生まれた。彼女はGille Brigte mac Fergusa of Gallowayとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女はスコットランドで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eve mac Fergus(은)는 1150년 영국 스코틀랜드 히그h랜드 레드카스틀에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 스컷랜드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Eve mac Fergus nasceu em 1150, em Redcastle, Highland, Escócia, United Kingdom. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway. Ela faleceu em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Eve mac Fergus родилась в 1150 г. в местности редкастл, хаиланд, Шотландия, Великобритания. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway. Она умерла в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Eve mac Fergus nació en 1150, en Redcastle, Highland, Escocia, Reino Unido. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Gille Brigte mac Fergusa of Galloway. Murió en Scotland.
